item.title: About
item.description: My name is Ravshan Rikhsiev. Right now an undergraduate student at the INHA university in Tashkent, stepping to my bright future step by step. Developing own products which will help make people’s life easier and much safer. Contribute me on GitHub.

item.title: Browser-in-the-Browser Attack
item.description: Hello folks, My name is Ravshan. I am an enthusiast in the cybersecurity field. I like researching and learning in this field. I hope this topic will help you to be more aware. The trend we know that cybercriminals are always inventing new ways to deceive users in order to obtain their credentials, secret keys, or other valuable information. In general, all these attacks are aimed at vigilant users, regardless of how sophisticated they become.

item.title: Cyberbullying is a Serious Problem
item.description: In today’s post I propose to continue talking about the current problems of young people in the digital environment and talk about a well-known, but no less common and painful topic - cyberbullying, to analyze its essence, causes and ways to protect it. Cyberbullying is very similar to its “offline bullying” - bullying or simply put, harassment. It is verbal abuse directed towards a digital user. It takes the form of threats, shame, criticism of appearance, intelligence, and so on.

item.title: What Is a SOC Analyst?
item.description: A SOC analyst is a type of cybersecurity professional who looks mainly at the inner workings of networks. They help prevent security breaches and, when attacks do occur, they work with other people to find out how attackers got in. Communication is also a big part of being a SOC analyst because they have to be able to explain what they see on their screens and why certain things are happening while they are watching it.
